# TACO — The A2A Construction Open-standard

> An open-source construction ontology layer built on the A2A protocol (Linux Foundation).
> Task types, data schemas, and agent discovery for construction AI.

## Docs

- [Introduction](https://taco-protocol.com/docs/intro): Overview of TACO — the problem it solves, what it adds to A2A, and a quick example.
- [Why TACO?](https://taco-protocol.com/docs/why-taco): Ecosystem positioning, comparison with raw A2A and proprietary solutions, who benefits.
- [Build Your First Agent](https://taco-protocol.com/docs/getting-started/build-agent): Step-by-step guide to building a TACO-compatible construction agent.
- [Integrate an Existing Platform](https://taco-protocol.com/docs/getting-started/integrate-platform): How to add TACO support to an existing construction platform via an agent sidecar.
- [Task Types](https://taco-protocol.com/docs/task-types): The typed vocabulary of construction workflows — takeoff, estimate, rfi-generation, and more.
- [Agent Card Extensions](https://taco-protocol.com/docs/agent-card-extensions): Construction-specific extensions to A2A Agent Cards for discovery by trade, CSI division, and more.
- [Data Schemas](https://taco-protocol.com/docs/schemas/): Typed JSON schemas for construction artifacts — BOM, RFI, Estimate, Schedule, Quote, Change Order.
- [SDK Guide](https://taco-protocol.com/docs/sdk): Python SDK reference for building TACO agents with `taco-agent`.
- [Security](https://taco-protocol.com/docs/security): Scope taxonomy, trust tiers, token delegation, and Agent Card security extensions.
- [Examples](https://taco-protocol.com/docs/examples): Example agents — estimating, RFI generation, supplier quote — with Docker Compose setup.

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/pelles-ai/taco
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/taco-agent/
- A2A Protocol: https://a2a-protocol.org
- License: Apache 2.0
